channel.title: Cybersecurity on CEDIS / UnB
channel.description: Recent content in Cybersecurity on CEDIS / UnB

item.title: Cibersecurity
item.description: Cybersecurity consists of a set of practices, technologies, and methodologies aimed at protecting systems, networks, and data against unauthorized access, cyberattacks, persistent threats, and security failures. Its central goal is to ensure the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of information, especially in increasingly complex and interconnected digital environments.

item.title: People
item.description: Full Professor at the University of Brasília (UnB), working in the undergraduate Software Engineering program and the Graduate Program in Applied Computing.

item.title: People
item.description: Associate Professor at the University of Brasília (UnB), working in the Software Engineering undergraduate program and the Graduate Program in Applied Computing with a focus on requirements, human aspects, and software engineering education.

item.title: 3SW: master's dissertation proposes adapting CIS Controls v8.1 to Brazilian public administration
item.description: Tássio Correia da Silva defended at UnB’s Electrical Engineering graduate program a professional master’s dissertation adapting the CIS Controls v8.1 model to mitigate threats to Web servers in the Brazilian public administration.

item.title: gov.br security: a dissertation evaluates additional authentication factors for Brazil's digital identity
item.description: Sandro Leite Furtado defended at UnB’s Applied Computing graduate program a dissertation identifying and evaluating additional security factors for the gov.br identity, implementing and testing Risk-Based Authentication (RBA) as a complementary layer.

item.title: Improving Digital Public Service for Citizens
item.description: Closed project focused on improving digital public services for citizens.

item.title: Human Factors in Cybersecurity
item.description: Ongoing project on human factors associated with cybersecurity.
